title: Compression: Pruning, Quantization, Distillation
concept: Model compression is a family of tricks for making neural networks smaller or faster, including pruning away less useful parts, storing weights with lower precision, and training compact students through distillation.

exercises.prompt: Recap: In distillation, what role does the teacher model play?

options.text: It guides a smaller student by showing how a strong model behaves.
It removes all labels from the dataset.
It guarantees the student will be larger than itself.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Distillation is one compression tool — but it is not the only one.

cards.title: Three ways to make the suitcase lighter
cards.body: If your suitcase is too heavy, you have options: remove less important items, replace bulky containers with lighter ones, or rewrite a long guidebook into a short summary. Model compression works with the same spirit.

cards.title: A small survey of compression
cards.body: Pruning removes weights or connections that matter less. Quantization stores numbers in lower precision so the model uses less memory and often runs faster. Distillation trains a smaller student to imitate a larger teacher. Different methods squeeze a model in different ways.

cards.title: Trim it, shrink it, or teach a shorter version
cards.body: Pruning is like cutting weak branches from a tree. Quantization is like switching from heavy glass jars to lighter containers. Distillation is like asking an expert to teach someone how to give the short version without losing the big ideas.

cards.title: One kiosk, three options
cards.body: Suppose a vision model works well on a desktop but must run on a small retail kiosk. A team might prune unimportant parts, quantize the weights to smaller number formats, or distill a new student model for the kiosk. All three aim at the same deployment problem from different angles.

cards.title: Compression is not one single trick
cards.body: A common myth is that compression just means chopping off layers. In reality, there are multiple families of methods, and they trade off effort, speed gains, and accuracy loss differently.

cards.title: Edit the model or train a new student
cards.body: Compression methods do not all work the same way.

left.text:
Squeeze the existing model.
Remove less useful parts or store numbers more compactly.
Often a direct way to cut size and latency.

right.text:
Train a new smaller student.
Use a strong teacher for guidance.
Useful when you want a compact model with teacher-like behavior.

exercises.prompt: What does pruning try to do?

options.text: Remove less important weights or connections from a model.
Replace all labels with unlabeled data.
Force every prediction to be slower but more dramatic.
Increase precision so the model stores larger numbers.

exercises.explain: Correct. Pruning aims to cut away parts that contribute less.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Quantization often means storing weights with lower numerical precision than the original model.
exercises.explain: True. Lower precision is one of the main ideas behind quantization.

exercises.sentence: Training a smaller model from a larger teacher is called ___.
exercises.explain: Distillation is correct. The student learns from the teacher’s behavior.

exercises.prompt: Which plan sounds most like quantization?

options.text: Store the model’s weights in a smaller number format to save memory and often speed up inference.
Use a teacher model to supervise a new student network.
Delete the dataset and hope the model compresses itself.

exercises.explain: Right. Quantization is about changing number precision, not teacher-student training.

exercises.prompt: Which option matches the three compression ideas correctly?

options.text: Pruning removes less useful parts, quantization uses lower precision, and distillation teaches a smaller student from a larger teacher.
Pruning adds more layers, quantization labels the dataset, and distillation removes all activations.
Pruning and quantization are identical, and distillation only applies to linear regression.
All three methods work by making the model bigger first.

exercises.explain: Exactly. That is the core survey: cut less useful parts, use lighter number formats, or teach a smaller student.
